01_initial_request.input_data: Synchrotron radiation, precursors for (BiO)2CO3 and ZnAPO-34 particles
01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop an in-situ combined technique to acquire simultaneously time-resolved information of hierarchical structure of a dynamic process without mechanical switching
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Time resolution reaching the level of seconds or sub-second level

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Synchrotron radiation techniques like SAXS, XRD, and XAFS exist separately for structural characterization. [Gap] Simultaneous acquisition of hierarchical structure information without mechanical switching is missing, leading to non-simultaneous and potentially contradictory data. [Decision] To achieve simultaneous measurement of local atomic, nanoscale, and microscale structures, chose to use SAXS/XRD/XAFS combined setup to perform assembly of detectors (Pilatus3S 1M, Mythen, Diamond detector) on beamline 1W2B, expecting to obtain a compact setup avoiding mechanical movement between modes.

parameters.detectors: Pilatus3S 1M (2D), Mythen (1D curved), Diamond detector (5 mm x 5 mm)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: A compact setup was constructed where the XAFS detector is coupled into the SAXS beamstop, allowing simultaneous scattering and spectral signal measurement without mechanical switching.

references.citation: Yao, L., et al. 2022, J. Synchrotron Rad.
references.relevance: Supports the development of the diamond detector coupled to beamstop [33]

references.citation: Liu, Y. P., et al. 2022, Nucl. Sci. Tech.
references.relevance: Supports the application of silicon PIN photodiode for high-frequency sampling [34]

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional data acquisition records signals in the time domain with cumulative counts, limiting time resolution. [Gap] High time resolution is needed without sacrificing signal-to-noise ratio. [Decision] To achieve high-frequency data collection, chose to use NI 9223 high-speed counter card to perform high-frequency sampling scheme in the frequency domain, expecting to obtain improved signal-to-noise ratio with shortened counting time.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Experimental data collected in the frequency domain allows increasing signal-to-noise ratio by increasing the number of repeated measurements within a short period.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional energy scanning is unidirectional and slow due to return time. [Gap] Efficiency of X-ray source utilization and time resolution need improvement for rapid processes. [Decision] To achieve higher time resolution, chose to use Si (111) monochromator to perform two-way energy scanning strategy (forward and backward flying mode), expecting to obtain continuous data acquisition with reduced invalid time.

parameters.direction: Forward (low to high) and Backward (high to low)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two-way energy scanning strategy allows repeated acquisition of real-time SAXS, XRD, and XAFS data with higher utilization rate of X-ray source.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two-way scanning is beneficial for rapid changes but generates large data volume. [Gap] Slow chemical reaction processes require different acquisition optimization. [Decision] To achieve suitable acquisition for slow processes, chose to use Si (111) monochromator to perform one-way energy scanning strategy (traditional step-scanning mode), expecting to obtain high-quality XAFS data for slow reactions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: One-way energy scanning strategy is more suitable for XAFS data acquisitions of slow chemical reaction processes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] In two-way scanning, angular positions are not recorded directly but derived from rotation time. [Gap] Incident X-ray energy must be restored as the abscissa of the absorption spectrum. [Decision] To achieve accurate energy calibration, chose to use Cu foil to perform standard sample measurement and derivative analysis, expecting to obtain conversion files from acquisition time to incident X-ray energy.

parameters.calibration_method: First derivative maximum to determine E0 and theta0

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Angular positions of all data points were determined according to rotation velocity, and conversion files from time domain to energy space were prepared.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-frequency sampling generates hundreds of thousands to millions of data points per spectrum. [Gap] Raw data needs reduction to normal spectrum size while maintaining quality. [Decision] To achieve usable XAFS spectra, chose to use Statistical averaging algorithm to perform segmentation and averaging of high-frequency data, expecting to obtain normal XAFS spectrum consisting of hundreds of data points.

parameters.segmentation: Divide spectrum into hundreds of segments
parameters.energy_span: Equal to or better than energy resolution of monochromator (eV level)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: High-frequency sampling XAFS spectrum was restored to a normal XAFS spectrum with good data quality after statistical averaging.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SAXS and XRD patterns collected during XAFS scan correspond to different incident X-ray energies. [Gap] Anomalous scattering effect near absorption edge affects averaging. [Decision] To achieve consistent SAXS/XRD patterns, chose to use Statistical averaging algorithm to perform energy calibration and exclusion of anomalous regions, expecting to obtain averaged SAXS or XRD pattern with same time resolution as XAFS.

parameters.exclusion_range: 20 eV lower than E0 to 200 eV higher than E0
parameters.interpolation: Interpolated to have same q interval

02_agent_trajectory.observation: SAXS, XRD, and XAFS data have the same time resolution and can be used for further structural analysis after preprocessing.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The combined technique and strategies are developed. [Gap] Feasibility and repeatability of the setup need verification with a stable sample. [Decision] To achieve validation of the technique, chose to use SAXS/XRD/XAFS combined setup to perform measurement on Cu powder, expecting to obtain reproducible SAXS, XRD, and XAFS data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Four consecutive XAFS spectra and corresponding SAXS/XRD patterns were identical, illustrating well the repeatability of the data.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The technique is validated on stable samples. [Gap] Capability to track dynamic processes in complex fluids needs demonstration. [Decision] To achieve in-situ monitoring of particle formation, chose to use In-situ sample cell to perform hydrothermal treatment of Bi-based precursor, expecting to obtain time-resolved structural evolution data of (BiO)2CO3 particles.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obvious changes with reaction time were found from SAXS, XRD patterns and XAFS spectra, reflecting the in-situ formation process.

references.citation: Liu, Y. P., et al. 2019, CrystEngComm
references.relevance: Supports the synthesis and formation mechanism of Bi-based particles [35]

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] (BiO)2CO3 formation was successfully monitored. [Gap] Applicability to other zeolite-like materials needs confirmation. [Decision] To achieve comparative validation, chose to use In-situ sample cell to perform hydrothermal synthesis of ZnAPO-34, expecting to obtain SAXS, XRD, and XANES data consistent with previous reports.

parameters.temperature_profile: 298 K to 443 K at 5 K/min, then held at 443 K

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Data characteristics of SAXS, XRD, and XANES are the same as previous reports, confirming suitability for in-situ process researches.

references.citation: Beale, A. M., et al. 2006, J. Am. Chem. Soc.
references.relevance: Comparison with combined SAXS/WAXS/XAFS setup for ZnAPO-34 [28-30]

time_resolution.interpretation: Time resolution reached the level of seconds (e.g., 8 s for Cu powder test), meeting the goal of seconds or sub-second level depending on X-ray flux.

signal_to_noise_ratio.interpretation: High-frequency sampling scheme at 100 kHz improved signal-to-noise ratio to about 300.

mechanical_switching.interpretation: No mechanical movement required during measurements except monochromator rotation, avoiding switching between setups.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. The novel SAXS/XRD/XAFS combined technique successfully acquired simultaneously time-resolved information of hierarchical structure without mechanical switching, with time resolution reaching the level of seconds.
